Though the ocean was reduced by him to a pitiable condition
being drained off, yet it was raised to celestial glory, as it exhibited
the glittering (or struggling) whales, water-elephants and serpents
as well as heaps of gems that were scattered about.
[The commentator interprets the first compound as applying
to the world of Gods as well : That world possesses denizens some
of whom move about riding whales, some others reside inside water,
some ride elephants, some move with retrograde motion and some
go about throwing gems.]
qfir: afcmq i
fsnrfe ^ IUII
Though the waters were taken away, still it came to possess,
as it were, the charm of a lake in autumn with its waves, white
lotuses and swans, as a result of its being filled with floundering
whales, pearl-oysters and conch-shells.
